overview: Removes target-language content from text units in a filter event pipeline. By default all targets on all text units are removed. You can restrict removal to specific text units by ID or preserve specific locale targets, and optionally remove the entire text unit if no targets remain.

tusForTargetRemoval.help:
Restricts target removal to only the listed text units. When left empty, targets are removed from **all** text units.

Provide a comma-separated list of text unit IDs (as they appear in the source document). Only effective when `filterBasedOnIds` is `true`.
tusForTargetRemoval.values: Comma-separated text unit IDs, e.g. `tu1,tu2,tu5`. Empty string means all text units.
examples: `tu1,tu2` — only remove targets from text units with IDs `tu1` and `tu2`
`` (empty) — remove targets from every text unit

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this parameter to take effect

targetLocalesToKeep.help:
Specifies which locale targets to **keep** (all others are removed). When left empty, all targets are kept by default (i.e. no locale-based filtering).

Only effective when `filterBasedOnIds` is `false`.
targetLocalesToKeep.values: Comma-separated locale codes, e.g. `fr-FR,de-DE`. Empty string means keep all locales (no filtering).
examples: `fr-FR,de-DE` — keep only French and German targets, remove all others

dependsOn.condition: must be false for this parameter to take effect

filterBasedOnIds.help:
Switches the filtering mode between ID-based and locale-based. The two modes are **mutually exclusive** — you cannot filter on both IDs and locales simultaneously.

- **true** (default): uses `tusForTargetRemoval` to select which text units have their targets removed.
- **false**: uses `targetLocalesToKeep` to decide which locale targets to preserve.

removeTUIfNoTarget.help: Controls whether a text unit should be completely removed from the event stream if it has no remaining targets after the removal process. Useful for cleaning up empty text units that would otherwise pass through as source-only entries.

limitations: The wiki notes no known limitations.
processingNotes: All target languages are removed by default — not just the current target language.
ID-based filtering and locale-based filtering are mutually exclusive; `filterBasedOnIds` controls which mode is active.

examples.title: Remove all targets from all text units
examples.description: Default configuration — removes every target from every text unit in the pipeline.

examples.title: Remove targets from specific text units only
examples.description: Only removes targets from text units with IDs `tu1` and `tu2`, leaving all other text units untouched.

examples.title: Keep only specific locales and clean up empty TUs
examples.description: Switches to locale-based mode, keeping only French and German targets. Text units left with no targets are removed entirely.
